explanation: Past time expressions like 'yesterday' require past tense. Use 'had' not 'have'.
'Had' never takes an 's'. It is the same for all subjects.
'Had' is already the past form. Never add '-ed' to 'had'.

correct: They had a lot of money.
explanation: 'Money' is uncountable — it has no plural form. Use 'a lot of money'.

wrong: We had a lot of informations.
correct: We had a lot of information.
explanation: 'Information' is uncountable — never used in plural form.

wrong: She has a car last year.
correct: She had a car last year.
explanation: For past time (last year), use 'had', not 'has'.

wrong: I had a pen since childhood.
correct: I have had a pen since childhood.
explanation: With 'since', use present perfect (have/has + had), not simple past 'had'.
With numbers more than one, the noun must be plural. 'Two brothers' not 'two brother'.
'Children' is already the plural of 'child'. No article 'a' with plurals.
Use 'many' with countable nouns (friends). Use 'much' only with uncountable nouns.
